page: Amazon Web Services g7e.12xlarge is a graphics-intensive server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores from an Intel Xeon 8559C processor running at 2.4 GHz, built on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 10.67 GB of RAM per core. For accelerated workloads, it includes two NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPUs with a combined 192 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of 3800 GB of NVMe SSD, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 400 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance in several categories. The instance achieves top-tier results in single-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench single-core scores, and Geekbench image processing. It also demonstrates top-tier performance in static web serving throughput and LLM inference speeds across small, medium, and large models for both prompt processing and text generation. Memory bandwidth tests and multi-core CPU benchmarks, including Passmark and Geekbench multi-core, consistently place in the strong category, while single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU performance falls within the average range.

Given its high-density hardware profile, which bundles dual Blackwell GPUs, substantial DDR5 memory, and fast local NVMe SSD storage, the g7e.12xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for resource-intensive deployments that would otherwise require multiple smaller nodes. The presence of advanced CPU instruction sets, such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and Advanced Vector Extensions, further enhances its capability for local AI workloads. This instance is highly suited for large language model inference, ray tracing, image processing, high-throughput web serving, and database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services g7e.12xlarge is a graphics-intensive server instance designed for high-performance computing and visualization workloads. Powered by the Nitro hypervisor, it features 48 vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8559C processor, 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory, and two NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPUs with 192 GB of total VRAM. Storage is supported by a 3800 GB local NVMe SSD, complemented by a 400 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmarks, the instance delivers top-tier performance in LLM inference, single-core processing, and image rendering, alongside strong memory bandwidth and multi-core execution. This hardware profile provides qualitative cost efficiency for dense, multi-resource workloads by consolidating compute, memory, and graphics acceleration. It is highly suited for large language model inference, ray tracing, database operations, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g7e.12xlarge is a graphics-intensive server featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, two NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs with 192 GB VRAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage for high-performance workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g7e.12xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server with 48 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, dual NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-density GPU instance featuring dual Blackwell accelerators, DDR5 memory, and local NVMe storage for advanced AI and graphics workloads.
bullet_points: Dual NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPUs with 192 GB total VRAM
48 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 512 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory
3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 400 Gbps network bandwidth
Top-tier benchmark performance in LLM inference and image processing
Best suited for LLM inference, ray tracing, and database operations
